description: In an office, Joanna stands looking at Rachel, who is seated at a desk. Joanna wears a black suit, and Rachel wears a gray suit. Rachel stands up, grabbing a silver bag from the desk. "Um," Rachel says.
Rachel turns away from the desk, saying, "well, I guess there's no use me sticking around to the end of the day."
Rachel walks toward the door. Joanna raises her hands and steps forward.
"Wait," Joanna says, her hands raised.
Rachel stops and turns her head to face Joanna.
Joanna lowers her hands and says, "You can put your sad little muffin back in its drawer."
Rachel steps back toward Joanna, holding her bag in front of her.
"Must know the truth," Joanna says, tilting her head slightly.
Joanna continues, "I didn't want to lose a perfectly good assistant," maintaining eye contact with Rachel.
Rachel's eyebrows draw together as she looks at Joanna.
"What?" Rachel asks.
Joanna gestures with both hands, saying, "That's why I said all those things about your flirting and your drinking."
Rachel stares at Joanna, her mouth slightly open.
"My drinking?" Rachel asks, her pitch rising on the question.
Joanna lowers her gaze and shifts her weight.
"I must have said that after you left," Joanna says, looking back up at Rachel.
Rachel steps closer, her eyes widened.
"Said what exactly?" Rachel asks, keeping her gaze fixed on Joanna.
"That you enjoyed the occasional drinking binge," Joanna replies, nodding slightly.
Rachel's mouth drops open.
"Oh, my God," Rachel says, raising her hands.
Rachel turns away, gripping her bag, and says, "Oh, that is it. I believe you are just a horrible person," as she walks toward the door.
Joanna follows, reaching out to grab Rachel's arm, saying, "Wait. Now, if you're going to get all sensitive about it..."
"...I don't want to lose you. What if I create a" Joanna continues, her hand remaining near Rachel's arm.
"...position for you? I'll make you assistant buyer in this department," Joanna says, stepping back and gesturing with her hands.
Rachel stares at Joanna, her expression still.
"Say more things like that," Rachel says.
Joanna raises one finger, looking at Rachel.
"you can have your own office and a raise effective tomorrow," Joanna says, motioning with her hands.
"I need an expense account," Rachel says.
Joanna looks steadily at Rachel.
"Done," Joanna replies quickly.
"And an assistant," Rachel says. "Sophie, get in here," Joanna shouts, her volume rising abruptly.
A woman in a green dress peers around the doorframe. The setting changes to a living room where Chandler sits in a brown recliner, facing a television.
On the television screen, a man's face is visible. A voice from the television says, "...now. It seems like a lifetime, doesn't it?"
Chandler sits in the recliner wearing a blue shirt. Ross sits in an adjacent recliner wearing a gray sweater. Joey walks through the background doorway wearing a dark jacket.
Joey walks into the room, setting a bag on the counter, and asks, "Hey. What are you guys doing here? I thought you'd be out partying with Gandalf."
Ross turns his head to look over his shoulder at Joey.
"It's Gandalf, and he's not coming," Ross says, turning his head back to face forward.
"So you've just been sitting around here all morning?" Joey asks, gesturing with his hands.
"No, I balanced my checkbook," Ross replies, leaning his head back against the chair.
Chandler raises a remote control and points it toward the television.
"Yeah, and I gave first names to all the foosball players," Chandler says, holding the remote forward.
Joey stands behind the recliners, looking between Ross and Chandler.
"I can't believe he didn't come," Ross says.
"So what if he didn't come? We could still go out and party ourselves," Joey says, opening his arms outward.
"I don't know. You," Chandler says, shifting his weight in the chair.
"...know, with Gandalf, we'd be out all night," Chandler continues, moving his hands as he speaks.
Ross raises his hands, his fingers spread wide.
"We'd meet total strangers and hang out with them," Ross says, moving his hands rapidly.
"We could do that," Joey replies, his arms returning to his sides.
Chandler and Ross sit silently in their respective recliners.
"There was other stuff, too," Chandler says.
Joey walks around the recliners, positioning himself in front of Ross and Chandler.
"We'll do it all and better," Joey says, pointing his finger toward them.
"Look, after tonight, Gandalf will want to party with us. Come on," Joey continues, motioning upward with his arms to encourage them to stand.
Ross leans forward and begins to rise from his recliner.
"Yeah," Ross says, reaching for his jacket.
Joey nods, watching Ross stand up.
"It's not like we don't know how to party," Ross says, putting on his jacket. "Yeah. All right, let's go," Joey agrees.
Chandler stands up and says, "And maybe we could end up on a boat again." "Yeah," Ross replies.
The three men walk past a foosball table toward the apartment door. Chandler stops and turns to face Ross and Joey.
"When were we on a boat?" Chandler asks.
"Remember that really cold morning you woke up and those dogs were licking your..." Ross says. "Yeah, yeah," Chandler interrupts.
Ross remains facing Chandler.
"Those were seals, man," Ross says.
Chandler's eyes widen slightly. He stares forward briefly before turning toward the door.
The men exit the apartment. The setting changes to a night view of a city skyline with a lit bridge. Then, the setting changes to an apartment interior where Rachel opens the front door and walks in.
Rachel walks in holding a brown suit on a hanger. "Hey, Mon, little question for you," she says.
"How do you think this suit would look on an assistant buyer?" Rachel continues. "The owner," Monica begins, standing near a kitchen table wearing a brown long-sleeve shirt.
"...Alessandros, came over here to yell at me, but instead, I made him some sauce," Monica says, gesturing with her hands.
"...and he offered me the job as head chef," Monica continues. "Oh, my God. You just..." Rachel says, her mouth opening.
"...ruined the thing I was practicing the whole way home," Rachel says, holding the suit out.
"But I'm so happy," Rachel adds, smiling.
Monica smiles widely, placing her hands flat against her chest.
"Can you believe it? I finally get to run my own kitchen," Monica says.
"Oh, you've waited so long," Rachel says, stepping forward to hug Monica.
"No," Monica says as the two women embrace.
In an apartment kitchen, Rachel holds a brown suit jacket on a hanger while Monica stands nearby. Phoebe walks into the kitchen wearing a black top with large red roses.
Rachel turns to Phoebe and says, "Hey, Phoebe, quick question for you."
"Yeah?" Phoebe replies. Rachel holds up the jacket and asks, "How do you think this suit would look on an assistant buyer at Bloomingdale's?"
Phoebe shrugs and gestures with one hand, saying, "I don't know. Totally depend on her coloring and"
Phoebe gasps, her eyes widening as she points at Rachel.
"you got the job," Phoebe shouts, jumping up and down with her volume raised. Monica leans forward and says, "You got the job? Why didn't you tell me?" Audience cheering and laughter are audible.
Rachel smiles widely and looks at Monica.
"It's going to be so great," Rachel says, her volume elevated.

Rachel gestures enthusiastically while holding the jacket. "I'm going to get to help decide what we sell. I'm going to have an office with walls and everything. I'm going to have walls," she says. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe smiles and spreads her arms.
"Is this the day of good news or what?" Phoebe says.
Phoebe points to herself and says, "I got us a job. The wedding reception."
Monica's smile fades as she says, "Oh," softly.
Monica lowers her hands and takes a step toward Phoebe.
"Phoebe. I kind of need to talk to you about that," Monica says to Phoebe.
Monica steps closer to the refrigerator, placing one hand on her chest.
"Well, I think it might be time for me to take a step back from the catering," Monica says, gesturing with both hands. Phoebe responds, "But we've only had one job."
Monica places her hands on her hips.
"I know, but now we have this second one, and it feels like it's snowballing," Monica says, making a rolling motion with her hands.
Phoebe looks at Monica, her expression changing as her smile disappears.
"Yeah. What are you saying?" Phoebe asks.
Monica turns her gaze toward Phoebe.
"I got offered the head chef job at Alessandro's," Monica says, gesturing outward.
"What?" Phoebe asks. Monica waves her hands and adds, "It's okay, because you know what? If you think about it, you don't really need me for the business." "You're the cook," Phoebe says.
Phoebe looks at Monica with wide eyes.
"Without you, it's just me driving up to people's houses with empty trays and asking for money," Phoebe says. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks down briefly and shifts her weight.
"All right," Monica says.
Monica gestures toward Phoebe with an open hand.
"well, I'll pay you back all the money you invested, and you can keep the van," Monica says. Phoebe asks, "For what?"
Phoebe turns away, crossing her arms.

"Can't believe this," Phoebe says, turning toward the door. "Got to get out of here," she adds, grabbing her coat. Monica calls after her, "Phoebe. Wait a minute," as Phoebe walks out of the kitchen. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stands alone in the living area, holding her hands clasped in front of her.
Rachel throws her arms up, looking upward, and shouts, "I'm an assistant buyer." Audience cheering and laughter are audible.

The setting changes to outside a storefront window with text reading "CENTRAL PERK". Upbeat acoustic guitar music plays briefly. Joey, Ross, and Chandler walk through the door into the coffee shop as ambient chatter is heard.
Joey waves a hand and says, "All right, so we'll get a little coffee, we'll get energized, and we'll head back out." Chandler murmurs, "All right," and Ross adds, "Okay," as they walk forward.
The three men sit heavily on an orange couch. Chandler sits on the right armrest, Ross in the middle, and Joey on the left.
Joey leans forward and says, "so we're having fun, right? We don't need that wizard guy," gesturing with one hand.
"We'll hit a couple of clubs, talk to some strangers, and after this, we'll go down to the docks and see about that boat thing," Joey says, looking between Ross and Chandler.
Ross looks forward, resting his hands on his lap.
"I'm kind of beat," Ross says.
Ross turns his gaze toward Joey.
"actually," Ross adds.
"Me, too," Chandler says.
Joey looks at Ross and Chandler.
"You serious?" Joey asks.
Ross nods slightly.
"Yeah," Ross replies. "Thank God. I'm exhausted," Joey says, leaning his head back onto the couch and looking toward the ceiling. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross and Chandler lean back on the couch, resting their heads and looking upward, appearing motionless.

A blond-haired man in a red shirt approaches with a notepad and asks, "So you guys want coffees?" Joey gestures and says, "Yeah, but I don't want to be up too late, so I'll have a decaf." Ross adds, "Yeah, me, too."
Chandler touches his neck and says, "Actually, could I get some hot water with a little lemon". Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler keeps his hand near his neck and turns toward Ross.
"strain? My voice screaming in there. Does it have to be so loud?" Chandler asks Ross.
Ross looks at Chandler, spreading his hands slightly.
"I can't hear a word you're saying. My ears are ringing so bad," Ross says. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures with one hand. "I'm just glad I brought that extra pair of socks," he says.
"I use them as mittens. I didn't want to touch a thing in that last place," Joey says, making a pushing motion with both hands. Audience laughter is audible.
The three men sit in silence, their expressions downcast.
Ross looks around and asks, "How sad are we?" Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks away, lowering his gaze.
"Yeah, I know," Joey says softly.
Chandler shifts on the armrest.
"You know, we're not sad. We're not sad. We're just not 21 anymore," Chandler says, gesturing with his hand.
Chandler points a thumb at his chest.

"I'm 29 years old, damn it. And I want to sit in a comfortable chair and watch television and go to sleep at a reasonable hour," Chandler says, his volume elevated. "Yeah," Joey replies. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures with one hand, leaning forward slightly.
"and I like to hang out in a quiet place where I could talk to my friends," Joey says. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks toward the others, raising his hands slightly.
"Yeah, and so what if I like to go home, throw on some Kenny G and take a bath?" Ross says, gesturing with an open hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Audience laughter fades as Ross holds his gesture, and Joey and Chandler look at him in silence.
In a coffee shop, Joey, Ross, and Chandler sit on a sofa.
"We're 29," Ross says.
"We're not women," Ross says.
Chandler and Joey look silently at Ross. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a view of a city skyline at sunrise over a body of water.
Back in the coffee shop, Phoebe sits on the sofa, placing a mug on the table. Monica walks over to her.
"Oh, there you are," Monica says, standing near the sofa.
Monica begins to sit down next to Phoebe.
"I'm glad that you decided to hear me out," Monica says as she settles onto the sofa.
"Okay, I'm hearing," Phoebe replies.
Monica pulls a small notepad and a pen from her jacket pocket.
"I've been doing a lot of thinking," Monica says.
"A lot," Monica adds.
Gesturing with the pen, Monica says, "And, well, I came up with a whole bunch of businesses that you can do with your van."
Monica flips open the notepad and looks at it.
"You could be a flower delivery person," Monica suggests.
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica, leaning forward slightly.
"What?" Phoebe asks.
"Or a bakery delivery person," Monica continues.
Phoebe raises her hand, pointing her finger upward.
"Pizza," Phoebe adds, gesturing with one hand.
Phoebe smiles, and Monica looks at her while holding the pen. Audience laughter is audible.
"All right, I got a whole bunch of stuff in this area, but I'm getting the feeling that you don't want to deliver," Monica says.
Phoebe shakes her head.
"No," Phoebe answers.
"Okay," Monica replies.
Monica crosses something out on the notepad while Phoebe watches with her arms crossed. Audience laughter is audible.
"And I'm guessing if you don't want to deliver, you probably don't want to pick up stuff either," Monica says, glancing at Phoebe.
Phoebe shakes her head again.
"No," Phoebe confirms.
Monica crosses more items off the notepad, her gaze directed downward. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks up and says, "You know what?"
Monica turns her gaze toward Phoebe.
"Let's do the catering business," Monica says. "Really?" Phoebe asks.
"Are you sure?" Phoebe asks, leaning forward.
"Yeah, I made a commitment to you, and it'd be fun," Monica says. "Oh, it will be fun," Phoebe replies.
Phoebe leans in toward Monica with her arms open.
Phoebe embraces Monica and says, "Yay."
They separate, and Monica says, "Okay, let's plan the wedding reception." Phoebe replies, "Okay," taking the notepad.
Phoebe holds the notepad and examines the pages.
"Wow," Phoebe says softly. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe flips the notepad page over.
"Really wanted me to do something with this van," Phoebe remarks, looking at the notes.
Monica looks at Phoebe. Phoebe hands the notepad back to Monica.
"You know what? I want you to take the chef job," Phoebe states, looking at Monica.
Monica's eyes widen as she looks at Phoebe.
"Really?" Monica asks.
"Yeah," Phoebe says. "That's what you really want?" Monica asks.
"Yeah. I don't want to be the reason you're unhappy. That would just make me unhappy. And I really don't want to be the reason I'm unhappy," Phoebe explains.
Monica smiles.
"Thank you," Monica says.
Monica and Phoebe embrace tightly.
"Besides, it might be kind of fun to form the new A team," Phoebe says.
Monica smiles at Phoebe.
The setting changes to an exterior view of tall city buildings with cars driving on a road.
In an office, Rachel enters through a door holding her coat, then sees Mrs. Lynch carrying a cardboard box filled with items.
"Oh, hi, Mrs. Lynch. Is Joanna in already?" Rachel asks. "Oh, my goodness. You haven't heard?" Mrs. Lynch replies. "Heard what?" Rachel asks.
"Joanna passed away last night," Mrs. Lynch states.
"Oh, my God. How?" Rachel asks, placing her hands on her chest.
"Well, she was leaving work and she was hit by a cab," Mrs. Lynch says. Audience laughter is audible.
"Oh, my God," Rachel says.
"Oh, I cannot believe it," Rachel says. "I know," Mrs. Lynch replies.
"Oh, God," Rachel says.
Rachel keeps her hands pressed to her chest.
"Oh, God," Rachel repeats.
Rachel maintains her hands on her chest.
"Oh, God," Rachel says again.
Mrs. Lynch looks at Rachel.
"I didn't realize that you were so close," Mrs. Lynch says.
Rachel looks down.
"Yes, so close," Rachel says, briefly covering her face with her hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers her hand and looks back up at Mrs. Lynch.
"Mrs. Lynch, I know that this is an emotional and difficult time for all of us, but by any chance, did Joanna" Rachel says, moving her hands as she speaks.
"send any paperwork your way before" Rachel continues.
Rachel keeps her hands clasped in front of her.
"it happened?" Rachel concludes.
Mrs. Lynch shakes her head slightly.
"No, nothing," Mrs. Lynch answers.
Rachel's eyes widen slightly.
"Imagine if she just stepped off that curb a few seconds," Mrs. Lynch says, looking downward.
Rachel steps closer to Mrs. Lynch.
"Yes, yes, just a few seconds and she'd still be with us," Rachel says, placing an arm around Mrs. Lynch's shoulder.
"Nothing about an assistant buyer?" Rachel asks softly, keeping her arm around Mrs. Lynch. Audience laughter is audible.
Mrs. Lynch begins to cry and pulls a tissue to her face.
"How'd you?" Mrs. Lynch says through her tears.
Rachel quickly removes her arm and steps away as Sophie enters the office.
"Good morning," Sophie says, smiling as she holds her jacket.
"Oh, Sophie. Guess you didn't hear about Joanna," Rachel says.
"I sure did," Sophie replies, smiling widely. Audience laughter is audible.
Sophie walks past Rachel, maintaining her smile as she heads toward her desk.

In a commercial kitchen, a man in a dark suit and red tie stands next to Monica, who wears a white chef's uniform and toque. They face a group of kitchen staff dressed in similar white chef uniforms. A prep table holding fresh produce sits in the foreground between them.
The man in the suit looks at the staff and says, "I'm so excited about having Monica come on board with us." Monica stands beside him with her hands clasped in front of her.
The kitchen staff listen silently, their arms crossed.
"Although I do feel bad about having fired Chef Emilio. It is like losing a member of the family. Of course," the man says, maintaining a serious expression as he looks around the room.
"that literally is the case for several of you," the man continues, gesturing toward the group. Audience laughter is audible.
The man shifts his gaze toward a specific group of staff members.
"Tony, Carlos, Marie, please tell your father how much we're going to miss him," the man says to a woman and two men standing together. Audience laughter is audible.
The three addressed staff members stand with their arms crossed, their lips pressed together in unsmiling expressions.
The man turns slightly toward Monica and says, "Now, I know that Monica has a lot of great ideas for this place." Monica smiles at the staff.
"Well, you all read the review, so without much further ado, I present to you," the man says, gesturing grandly toward Monica.
"our new head chef," the man concludes. He pats Monica on the shoulder and turns to walk away.
The man walks out of view. Monica steps closer to the prep table, reaching into her uniform pocket. The staff members maintain their silent, still stances.
Monica unfolds a small piece of paper and says, "You."
Monica looks down at the paper in her hands.
Reading from the paper, Monica says, "I just want to say that with a pinch of excitement, a dash of hard work, and a dollop of." She gestures slightly with her free hand.
"cooperation, we can have the recipe," Monica says, looking up from the paper with a wide smile.
Monica's smile slowly fades as she looks around at the unresponsive staff members, who continue to stare at her with crossed arms and stony expressions.
Monica lowers the paper slightly and asks, "Are you going to kill me?" Her volume is noticeably softer than her previous line.
The kitchen staff maintain their unmoving stares at Monica. Audience laughter is audible. The screen turns black.
The screen remains black.
The screen continues to remain black.
